floor_history.headline: fable-blitz deep recon: csmd4 IS marionation region-1's cheaper twin (no region-3) - the whole gap is the in-place-basin v1/a0 allocation-equation flip (pri(arg5) > pri(t0)); the wrap-weighting x in-p

Structural axis on csmd4 h5 base fully closed: 13 variants swept (var-splits, expression compression, decl-position, pointer-deref form) — 8 INERT at masked 2, 5 measured regressions (t0s-split=3, arg
Frontier top-3 cleared: do-while(0) wrap direction-invariant +2 insns (KILLS pri-lift lever), cnt-carrier regresses to masked 4, volatile fmt-carrier policy-rejected. h5 floor holds at 2.
s5 permuter: all 3 frontier hypotheses (block-scope multi-set carrier, named-dispatch reorder, nested do-while(0)) KILLED with measurements; h5 masked-2 floor holds

s11 structural: frontier M1 arg5_addr multi-set on p107 CLOSED across three C-realizations (algebraic-cancel +9, opaque-carrier +27, simple two-SET INERT via combine-fold) — combine.c substitution fol

frontier.hypothesis: decomp.me corpus scrape via tools/decomp_me_scrape.py against asm/funcs/cpu_side_move_dir_4.s may surface a structurally distinct C form from the BB2 toolchain class (gcc2.7.2-psx / gcc2.7.2-cdk / psyq3.5).
frontier.mechanism: Coarse asm-overlap pre-filter; manual inspection of top hits could reveal an original-source shape our search space missed. Blocked s9/s11 since curl_cffi module unavailable in .venv.
frontier.next_probe: Operator installs curl_cffi in .venv (`source .venv/bin/activate && pip install curl_cffi`). Then rederive session runs `python3 tools/decomp_me_scrape.py search --asm-file asm/funcs/cpu_side_move_dir_4.s` and manually inspects top asm-overlap hits.

frontier.hypothesis: The h5 masked-2 residual mechanism (insn 121 LAUNCH via single-SET p107) is inherent to any C form that expresses arg5_addr as a compound expression; the only C-source lever that could suppress insn 121's LAUNCH would be to make p107 die BEFORE the sched2 window OR to make the SET dest itself a multi-set at combine-time (not at C-source-decomposition-time).
frontier.mechanism: combine.c's substitution runs BEFORE flow.c recomputes reg_n_sets; only p101 (t0) achieves genuine multi-set via direct in-place C respellings (t0 <<= 2 or t0 += ...). No analogous C form exists for arg5_addr that resembles a genuine in-place update on a single pseudo (because arg5 is a value, not a mutable pointer being walked). Consider a pointer-shaped arg5_addr walker construct.
frontier.next_probe: Draft a pointer-walker form for arg5_addr - e.g. `s32 *ap = (s32 *)(s32)tbl_125c; ap = (s32 *)((s32)ap + v0); arg5 = *ap;` - test whether the pointer-typed local escapes cse's plus-fold. If also folded, frontier #2 dies; if not, potential mechanism hit for p_ap multi-set.

frontier.hypothesis: arg5 memory-read is being LAUNCHED as insn 121 (a PLUS SET birthing p107); an alternate mechanism to suppress the LAUNCH is to route the memory read through a pseudo that is NOT the PLUS SET dest - e.g., let expand emit a MEM using an address computed as a subexpression without a distinct pseudo carrier for the sum.
frontier.mechanism: expand.c emits (set dst (mem (plus base offset))) directly for `*(T*)(a+b)` when no intermediate is named - the (plus base offset) is a subexpression of the MEM's address, NOT a separate SET. In that path, no p107 for the sum exists; the sched.c LAUNCH candidate is the MEM SET itself. This is the h5 baseline shape - so mechanism #3 is the untested INVERSE: force the sum to be a SET that DIES BEFORE the memory read via a manual liveness split.
frontier.next_probe: Draft `s32 a5 = v0 + (s32)tbl_125c; { s32 sink = a5; (void)sink; } arg5 = *(s32*)a5;` - test whether the (void)sink read materializes as a live use that dies before the MEM, altering the LAUNCH candidate list. Risk: dead-code eliminated by GCC before flow.
